page: Microsoft Azure E8_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Ev5 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks place the E8_v5 consistently in the average (middle 50%) category across most standard tests. This includes stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU metrics. Memory bandwidth tests, such as read-only small block cached (416.84 GB/sec) and large block uncached (59.7 GB/sec), also fall within the average tier. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium (7B) model LLM inference speeds are weak (bottom 25%), indicating potential bottlenecks during highly parallelized decompression or larger model inference tasks.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E8_v5 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing or local disk storage. The presence of hardware flags like AVX-512 VNNI and SHA_NI supports cryptography and vector operations. This server is appropriate for relational database hosting, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and software compilation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E8_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores) operating at 2.8 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance includes no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most CPU, memory, and database operations, though multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM inference speeds fall into the bottom 25%. Supported by CPU flags like AVX-512 VNNI and SHA_NI, the E8_v5 is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and average performance across standard database and memory benchmarks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and average benchmark performance for databases and caching.
tagline: An x86 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for database operations and caching workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
No bundled local storage or GPU resources
Average performance in database operations, Redis SET, and memory bandwidth

Suited for relational databases, caching layers, and static web serving
